purpose: Study the completed crack-repair Waves 1-11 and extract the exact build workflow that should be copied for future implementation waves.
source_authority_refs: git show --name-only for Wave 1-11 implementation and prep commits

repo_index_status_during_forensics.reason: Current repo-index staleness was caused by the committed build protocol update and had no missing tracked files or hash drift requiring discovery refresh for this forensic pass.

core_conclusion: The successful Waves 1-11 workflow was not automatic index refresh after every wave. The stable pattern was readiness/prep when ambiguity existed, owner-surface implementation, focused owner validation and pytest, implementation commit, and conditional index refresh only for discovery/reconciliation waves or closeout gates.

golden_wave_loop.name: readiness_question
golden_wave_loop.rule: Before implementation, ask whether the selected wave is ready or whether a small prep note would make future slices easier.
golden_wave_loop.wave_1_11_evidence: The operator repeatedly used readiness prompts before Waves 3-11. Prep commits were created only when they narrowed ambiguity or captured future-wave requirements.

golden_wave_loop.name: targeted_prep_when_needed
golden_wave_loop.rule: If readiness review finds ambiguity, update the active owner plan with compact preimplementation notes and rebuild plan-note index once. Do not create broad architecture reports.
wave_1_11_evidence: e5ec079d Record Wave 3 overlay taxonomy plan
cae85bca Record Wave 4 bridge boundaries
551caa55 Record Wave 5 research mode boundaries
f0463545 Record Wave 6 VBT642 mode plan
a31f2b97 Record future wave repair details
2e1bb0f5 Detail Wave 7 helper plan
11f018fd Record Wave 8 refresh safety sequence
58e5052f Document Wave 10 reusable no-write harness
4d563266 Add post Wave 11 closeout plan
9dbd6a35 Add post Wave 11 closeout checklist

golden_wave_loop.name: owner_surface_implementation
golden_wave_loop.rule: Implement the thin owner slice named by the plan: config/policy, schema, contract, module when needed, validator, and focused tests. Keep runtime-affecting values in config.
golden_wave_loop.wave_1_11_evidence: Waves 1-7 and 9-11 each committed a coherent owner bundle. Wave 8 was explicitly a helper/index reconciliation bundle.

golden_wave_loop.name: future_wave_notes_at_closeout
golden_wave_loop.rule: During implementation closeout, record only discoveries that affect later waves: implementation order, owner files, validators, full-read requirements, safety boundaries, index requirements, or activation gates.
golden_wave_loop.wave_1_11_evidence: Each completed wave in the repair execution plan carries future_wave_details or future_wave_implementation_details that guided later waves without reopening broad planning.

golden_wave_loop.name: focused_proof
golden_wave_loop.rule: Prove the wave with changed JSON parse, the owning validator, focused pytest, read-only/worktree checks where relevant, and git diff --check. Do not treat index freshness as slice proof unless the wave owns index/reconciliation.
golden_wave_loop.wave_1_11_evidence: The post-Wave-11 receipt recorded 19 focused validation commands, 9 focused pytest groups, 87 focused tests, a 24-command read-only harness, and no new root problem.

golden_wave_loop.name: implementation_commit
golden_wave_loop.rule: Stage exact intended paths and commit the coherent owner implementation after focused proof passes.
wave_1_11_evidence: 976e0cde Implement Wave 1 activation enforcement
377d6b6b Implement Wave 2 source acquisition semantics
eb3f3707 Implement Wave 3 runtime research overlay
a69d2cea Implement private VBT Data bridge wave
59d1c66e Implement private portfolio optimizer research modes
b714f9fe Implement VBT642 mode-aware matrix
f8399d3a Add shared VBT tool boundary helper
44987788 Reconcile Wave 8 helper and index discovery
a2c31d7c Add Wave 9 MCP runtime precedence gate
e1d27aba Add Wave 10 read-only command harness
413b07fc Implement Wave 11 legacy authority cleanup

golden_wave_loop.name: conditional_index_refresh
golden_wave_loop.rule: Refresh repo/script/config/output indexes only when the selected wave owns discovery/reconciliation, stale discovery blocks the next action, the next wave truly depends on fresh discovery, a release/closeout gate requires freshness, or the operator asks.
wave_1_11_evidence: Wave 8 explicitly owned helper manifest and script surface reconciliation, so it refreshed script-import, config inventory, output schema, and repo-index outputs.
Wave 10 and Wave 11 used discovery refreshes because their work was read-only/index mutation cleanup and scanner/exact-path cleanup.
Post-Wave-11 closeout refreshed full discovery indexes as a closeout gate.

golden_wave_loop.name: do_not_chase_generated_index_head_staleness
golden_wave_loop.rule: After a generated-index commit, treat head-only index staleness as evidence unless another required freshness condition exists.
golden_wave_loop.wave_1_11_evidence: Wave 10 explicitly told Wave 11 to treat repo-index and script-import head-only staleness as evidence until an explicit index-refresh task was requested.

per_wave_evidence.title: Activation schema, validator, and tests
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Direct implementation was acceptable because the active crack-repair plan already contained enough detail. Future-wave notes were saved in the active plan during implementation.

per_wave_evidence.title: Source registry live and historical acquisition semantics
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: A broader source-acquisition slice still stayed coherent because ownership was explicit and tests were focused around no-network/live-source semantics.

per_wave_evidence.title: Cross-policy private runtime research overlay validator
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: When semantics were subtle, a prep taxonomy was saved before code. The implementation then used hard_failure/research_gap/warning/pass classifications instead of vague blockers.

per_wave_evidence.title: VBT Data acquisition bridge
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Prep captured boundaries, then implementation kept the bridge thin and avoided provider execution, portfolios, and promotion claims.

per_wave_evidence.title: Portfolio and optimizer private research modes
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Prep emphasized status separation. Implementation avoided implying production readiness and kept acquisition, promotion, portfolio, and optimizer statuses separate.

per_wave_evidence.title: VBT642 mode-aware matrix and queue metadata
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Extra prep was useful before a matrix-wide semantic change. The implementation still stayed on the owner bundle and focused tests.

per_wave_evidence.title: Shared VBT tool boundary helper
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Shared helper work was planned only after Waves 3-6 proved repeated semantics. It did not start with a premature generic abstraction.

per_wave_evidence.title: Helper manifest and script surface reconciliation
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Index refresh was correct here because Wave 8 owned helper/script reconciliation and future waves needed fresh discovery baselines.

per_wave_evidence.title: MCP/runtime precedence tests
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Wave 9 reused the Wave 8 discovery baseline and focused on runtime precedence rather than refreshing indexes again.

per_wave_evidence.title: Read-only and index mutation cleanup
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Read-only harness semantics were planned before implementation. Discovery refresh was reasonable because the next scanner/exact-path wave depended on current script/index evidence.

per_wave_evidence.title: Scanner, exact-path, and legacy authority cleanup
per_wave_evidence.workflow_lesson: Scanner/exact-path cleanup correctly treated stale evidence as warnings unless a focused validator/test failed. It ended with planned closeout rather than inventing Wave 12.

closeout_evidence.future_follow_up_policy: Create a separate tracked follow-up only for a new root problem with owner surfaces, required validators, and focused tests.

rules_to_copy_for_future_waves: Start each wave with a readiness check and read the full active owner plan for that wave chain.
Create a prep note only when it narrows ambiguity or carries future-wave implementation details.
Implement one coherent owner bundle rather than mixing future placeholders into the slice.
Use focused owner validator and focused pytest as proof; index freshness is not proof unless index/reconciliation is the wave.
Record future-wave details at closeout only when they change order, owners, validators, full-read requirements, safety boundaries, index requirements, or activation gates.
Refresh indexes only for explicit discovery/reconciliation work, closeout gates, stale discovery blockers, true next-wave dependency, or operator request.
If indexes are refreshed, batch all required generated indexes from the same implementation HEAD and commit generated outputs once.
Do not chase head-only staleness caused by the generated-index commit.
workflow_to_avoid: Creating a repo-index refresh commit after every plan or packet edit.
Refreshing script-import and repo-index in alternating commits without a real discovery dependency.
Using index status as the proof that a source/runtime wave is correct.
Running broad validators repeatedly when the owning validator and focused pytest already prove the slice.
Leaving future-wave discoveries only in chat.
